af_score_components.notes: AI-driven trend monitoring platform with MCP support (added at v4.0.0). Has MCP quality 62. API keys required for AI providers (DeepSeek, OpenAI, Gemini) and notification channel tokens. Self-hosted, GPL-3.0. Primary audience appears Chinese-speaking. Documentation complexity is high due to 9 notification channels needing setup before MCP works. auth_complexity=85 for env-var API key pattern.

security_score_components.security_notes: Self-hosted platform — security depends on deployment environment. API keys for third-party AI providers managed via environment variables (reasonable). No formal CVE process as community project. GPL-3.0 license. HTTPS for external API calls. No compliance certifications. Notification channel tokens (Telegram, Slack, WeChat) add attack surface. Community project security review is limited.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Self-hosted so reliability is entirely user-managed. v6.0.0 shows active development. MCP support added at v4.0.0 — relatively new feature with potential rough edges. No SLA. Version history available via GitHub releases. Docker deployment provides some operational consistency. Dependent on external AI provider availability.

af_score_components.notes: IMPORTANT: trigger-dev is an agent/workflow execution platform, not an MCP server. Agents are built ON TOP of Trigger.dev, not through it as an MCP. No MCP server. Project-scoped API keys (simple). TypeScript/JavaScript only. Excellent documentation for durable execution, retries, and human-in-the-loop. auth_complexity=100 for simple API key auth.

security_score_components.security_notes: All HTTPS enforced. Project-scoped API keys with good isolation between projects. Open-source codebase enables security review. Self-hostable option for sensitive workloads. Environment variable secret management. No compliance certifications publicly listed but enterprise tier available. Active security maintenance on GitHub.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Durable execution is the core value proposition — automatic retries built in. Idempotency fully supported. Status page for cloud tier. Semantic versioning. SDK maintained actively. Self-hosting introduces own reliability concerns. Excellent error recovery design: retries, queues, human-in-the-loop pauses all built in. One of the strongest reliability stories for agent workflows.

af_score_components.notes: Turso edge SQLite platform REST API. No MCP server. Two-tier auth adds complexity: platform tokens for management, database-specific JWT tokens for data access. Agents must provision database tokens before querying newly created databases. Good documentation. SQLite limitations documented. auth_complexity=55 for two-tier auth requiring token management across both tiers.

security_score_components.security_notes: All HTTPS enforced. Excellent security model: two-tier auth with platform tokens (management) and short-lived database JWTs (data access). Database tokens support expiration and read-only scoping. SOC2 certified. Per-database isolation is a strong security feature for multi-tenant apps. Token rotation supported. Data encryption at rest.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: SOC2 certified cloud platform with status page. Global edge replication with eventual consistency. Agents must handle read-your-writes inconsistency (use primary URL directly after writes). No webhooks — requires polling for provisioning completion. SQLite single-writer limitation affects write-heavy agents. OpenAPI spec available.

af_score_components.notes: Turso MCP server for edge SQLite database management. Has MCP quality 80. Good tool coverage for database CRUD, schema management, and agent state storage. JWT-based database tokens with expiration. Multi-language SDK support. Per-database token management adds some complexity. auth_complexity=70 for API key + per-database JWT pattern.

security_score_components.security_notes: All HTTPS enforced. API token for platform, per-database JWT tokens for data access — good security isolation. SOC2 certified. Database tokens support expiration and read-only scoping. Short-lived tokens are a security best practice. MCP server is relatively new and security review is ongoing.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: SOC2 certified platform. MCP server is newer and may have coverage gaps. Embedded replica sync delay needs handling. Database branching experimental. SQLite single-writer limitation applies. Platform reliability is solid; MCP layer adds some uncertainty as relatively new addition.

af_score_components.notes: Twilio communications platform. No MCP server. Account SID + Auth Token as HTTP Basic Auth (simple but slightly dated). Comprehensive documentation for SMS, voice, WhatsApp, email. HIPAA/SOC2/GDPR compliant. Trial accounts limited to verified numbers — can break agent testing. US A2P 10DLC registration required for business SMS. auth_complexity=70 for Basic Auth pattern.

security_score_components.security_notes: All HTTPS enforced. HTTP Basic Auth with Account SID + Auth Token — functional but credentials are base64-encoded. API keys available for more granular access. HIPAA eligible, SOC2 Type II, GDPR compliant. Webhook signature validation (X-Twilio-Signature) for security. Phone number verification adds identity security. Enterprise security practices well-documented.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Twilio has public status page and enterprise SLAs. Industry-leading uptime for communications APIs. REST API versioned and stable for years. Retry guidance well-documented. Error codes comprehensive and consistent. Idempotency partially supported. Rate limits documented with clear retry-after headers. One of the most reliable communications APIs available.

af_score_components.notes: Twilio Video API for WebRTC-based video conferencing. No MCP server. Two-layer auth: Account SID + Auth Token for REST API, short-lived JWT Access Tokens for client-side (VideoGrant). Agents create/manage rooms server-side; cannot join video rooms server-side (WebRTC client only). auth_complexity=60 for dual-layer auth with JWT generation requirement.

security_score_components.security_notes: All HTTPS/WSS enforced. Access tokens are short-lived JWTs with VideoGrant scoping — excellent security for client-side access. HIPAA eligible, SOC2, GDPR compliant. Auth Token never exposed to clients. Webhook signature validation available. Room-level permission scoping. Twilio's infrastructure security is enterprise-grade.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Twilio status page covers Video API. Enterprise SLA. Access tokens expire (1hr default) — agents must handle refresh for long sessions. Room must be created before clients join (orchestration order matters). Recording requires separate enable call. Group Rooms limited to 50 participants. Strong platform reliability backed by Twilio infrastructure.

af_score_components.notes: Twitch Helix API for live streaming data. No MCP server. Both Client-ID and Authorization headers required for every request — missing one gives misleading 401 errors. App access tokens via client_credentials flow for server-to-server. OAuth2 for user-context actions. EventSub webhooks require public HTTPS endpoint. auth_complexity=60 for OAuth2 + dual-header requirement.

security_score_components.security_notes: All HTTPS enforced. OAuth2 with granular scopes for user actions. App access tokens for read-only server-to-server calls. Client secret never exposed in client-side code. EventSub requires webhook signature verification (Twitch-Eventsub-Message-Signature). GDPR compliant. Token refresh handled via OAuth flow. Strong scope granularity for user permissions.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Twitch has status page. Rate limits vary per endpoint with documented point costs. App access tokens must be refreshed after expiry. EventSub provides reliable webhook delivery with retries. Helix is the current stable API (Kraken v5 deprecated). Reasonable reliability for gaming/streaming integration. No MCP server available.

af_score_components.notes: Twitter/X API v2. No MCP server. Dual auth: Bearer token for read-only, OAuth 2.0 PKCE for posting (user context). Free tier is write-only — nearly useless for agents needing to read data. Aggressive pricing since 2023. Rate limits per 15-minute window tracked via response headers. auth_complexity=55 for OAuth2 PKCE requirement plus tier restrictions.

security_score_components.security_notes: All HTTPS enforced. OAuth 2.0 with fine-grained scopes (tweet.read, tweet.write, dm.read, etc.). Bearer tokens for app-only read access. PKCE flow for user context prevents secret exposure in client apps. GDPR compliant. Client secret secured server-side. Token revocation supported. Good security design despite platform controversies.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Twitter/X has had significant reliability issues since 2022 acquisition. Rate limit changes have been abrupt and undocumented. v1.1 deprecation ongoing with shifting timelines. Free tier restrictions make production use unreliable for most agent use cases. API availability has degraded since platform changes. 15-minute rate limit windows complicate agent retry logic.

af_score_components.notes: Typeform conversational forms API. No MCP server. OAuth2 for third-party integrations, personal access tokens for server-side agents. Webhook payload validation via Typeform-Signature. File upload responses return temporary URLs that expire — agents must download promptly. Complex form schema with many block types. auth_complexity=75 for bearer token simplicity in agent use.

security_score_components.security_notes: All HTTPS enforced. OAuth2 with scoped access (forms:read, forms:write, responses:read, etc.). Personal access tokens via Authorization header. Webhook signature verification (Typeform-Signature). GDPR compliant with data processing agreements. SOC2 certified. Response data encrypted. Good security posture for a forms platform handling sensitive survey data.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Typeform has status page. SOC2 certified. Response limits on all plans — agents must monitor usage. Cursor pagination for responses. Retry guidance in docs. API has been stable. Webhook delivery reliable with retry mechanism. File upload temporary URLs require prompt handling. Good overall reliability for forms use cases.

af_score_components.notes: Typesense open-source typo-tolerant search engine. No MCP server. API key via X-TYPESENSE-API-KEY header (simple). Scoped search-only keys generated from admin key for client-side. Self-hosted or Typesense Cloud. Excellent documentation. Vector search + hybrid search supported. OpenAPI spec available. auth_complexity=90 for simple header API key pattern.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced for Typesense Cloud (self-hosted security is user-managed). Scoped API key generation for granular access control — admin key generates search-only or collection-specific keys. Admin key protected server-side. GDPR and SOC2 compliant on Typesense Cloud. Open-source codebase enables security auditing. Regular releases with security fixes.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Typesense Cloud has status page and SLA. Self-hosted reliability is user-managed. Semantic versioning. Active development with regular releases. Schema changes require collection recreation (no in-place migration) — agents must handle this operational requirement. Retry guidance documented. Error messages clear and actionable. Multi-node clustering supported for HA.
